title: Value Chain Research: Driving Intelligence, Demand, & Value for Value Chain Leaders (IBM Proposal)
author: Aberdeen Group (Peter S. Kastner et al.)
abstract: Aberdeen Group formal research-sponsorship proposal to IBM covering Supply Management and Supply Chain/Fulfillment research agendas for 2004. Packages 10 research program sponsorships ($250K) + 10 customer case studies ($250K) + joint On Demand Supply Management workshop ($25K) with discount pricing to $125K total. Introduces Enterprise Research Alliances (ERA) with Penton Media, iSource, IndustryWeek and cites ~2,000-3,200 downloads per report. Flag: Aberdeen-Confidential / IBM-commercial-proposal.
importance_rationale: Documents Aberdeen's canonical mid-2000s research-sponsorship commercial model with explicit pricing ($250K/report, 50% bundle discount) and ERA-media partnership mechanics — rare primary evidence of analyst-firm economics.
relevance_rationale: Commercial pricing is historical but the research-sponsorship-plus-case-study bundle remains the core B2B analyst-firm revenue pattern; ERA media-alliance model persists in modified form with digital publications.
prescience_rationale: Proposal rather than forward-looking analysis; predictions limited to Q1-Q3 2004 research pipeline.
